dialogues.text: Beyond the fight for independence, the Katipunan, under Bonifacio's guidance, was built on a foundation of specific ideals and principles aimed at forging a new Filipino identity.
We are the Anak ng Bayan – the Children of the Nation. We join not just to fight, but to build a society based on equality, justice, and love for our homeland.
Remember the teachings: 'Love your country after God.' 'Banish from your thoughts all selfishness; think only of the common good.'
Bonifacio believed that true liberation involved not just political freedom, but also the enlightenment of the masses. He emphasized education as a crucial tool for empowerment.
Knowledge is our shield against deception. Learn to read, learn to think. Understand why we fight, and what kind of nation we wish to build. A free mind is the first step to a free Philippines.
The Katipunan fostered a strong sense of brotherhood and mutual support, ensuring that members looked after one another in times of hardship.
Supremo, the way our brothers help each other… it shows the strength of our unity. This is more than just a revolution; it is a movement of true solidarity.
Exactly, Pio. We are a family. We fight together, and we rise or fall together. Our bond is our greatest weapon against those who seek to divide us.

scenes.name: The Vision of a New Nation

dialogues.text: Bonifacio envisioned a Philippines free from colonial rule, where Filipinos governed themselves with justice, equality, and national pride.

We fight to break the chains, yes. But we also fight to build something new. A nation where every Filipino is treated with dignity, where our culture is honored, and where our children can grow in freedom and prosperity.

The ideals of the Katipunan, championed by Bonifacio, were not merely about overthrowing a colonizer, but about establishing a just and sovereign nation. This vision fueled the revolution and inspired generations to come.
